title: Half-Baked Product
author: weli
relevanceReason: 文章讨论了产品开发过程中的'半成品'概念，涉及软件开发、产品迭代和工程实践，与科技和开发相关。
summaryZh: 探讨产品开发中'半成品'状态的价值与意义
分析快速迭代与产品完善之间的平衡策略
highlightsZh: 讨论MVP（最小可行产品）与半成品的区别
分享产品开发过程中的实践经验
探讨技术产品的迭代策略
分析用户反馈对产品完善的影响

comments.author: TrackerFF
comments.textRawHtml:
One important part of the story is in the very beginning: The founder’s motivation. To become wealthy.

You see this in the startup world a lot. Founders with 5+ failed startups in different sectors, because said founder picked the fields mainly by doing some market analysis. Not domain expertise.

There’s then a big mismatch between what the founder thinks is possible, and what the domain expert thinks is possible.

The defense is of course that some people can do that - Musk did it, so why not?

Another defense is that blindingly naïve optimism is sometimes needed to move the needle, as the concept “that can’t be done” simply doesn’t exist to some people.

I’ve sat through some pitches like that, where it is very obvious that the founder/CEO has limited knowledge and expertise in what they’re pitching, where the product is limited, but their enthusiasm is off the charts.

EDIT: The very latest happened only a couple of weeks ago. A startup had reached out to my employer as they’re developing a platform in our domain. Higher ups liked what they’d seen, enough to arrange a real meeting.

Startup is only 3 months old, and the moment I opened the platform I recognized a vibecoded (likely using clause) platform identical to almost all other launched on a daily basis.

So I probed a bit about data sources, serious questions regarding security, etc. but the guy was pretty fluent in consultant (turns out he had worked as a management consultant before launching), and the CTO was just nodding along.

In the end they wanted our data, and promised the moon on features - but as mentioned, I’m sure the whole product was entirely vibecoded.
comments.textZhHtml:
故事的一个重要部分就在一开始：创始人的动机。致富。

这种情况在创业界屡见不鲜。创始人在不同行业有超过 5 家失败的初创公司，因为创始人主要通过做一些市场分析来选择领域。不是领域专业知识。

创始人认为可能的事情与领域专家认为可能的事情之间存在很大的不匹配。

辩解当然是有些人可以做到这一点 - 马斯克做到了，那么为什么不呢？

另一种辩解是有时需要令人盲目的天真乐观来推动发展，因为“不可能做到”的概念对某些人来说根本不存在

我参加过一些这样的推介，很明显，创始人/首席执行官对他们所推介的内容的知识和专业知识有限，产品也有限，但他们的热情却很高。

编辑：最新的情况发生在几周前。一家初创公司联系了我的雇主，因为他们正在我们领域开发一个平台。高层很喜欢他们所看到的，足以安排一次真正的会议。

初创公司只有 3 个月大，当我打开平台的那一刻，我认出了一个与几乎所有其他每天推出的平台相同的 vivicoded（可能使用条款）平台。

所以我探讨了一些关于数据源、有关安全的严重问题等。点头同意。

最后他们想要我们的数据，并承诺在功能上满载而归 - 但正如前面提到的，我确信整个产品完全是通过 vivicode 编码的。

children.author: aryehof
children.textRawHtml:
> pretty fluent in consultant

Thanks, gave me a chuckle. One to remember.
children.textZhHtml:
> 顾问非常流利

谢谢，让我笑了。一个要记住的。

children.author: thih9
children.textRawHtml:
> they wanted our data, and promised the moon on features

I know a company that fits this so well that I almost want to check the names. It's older than 3 months though.
children.textZhHtml:
> 他们想要我们的数据，并承诺在功能上给予好评

我知道一家非常适合这一点的公司，我几乎想检查一下名字。不过已经超过 3 个月了。

children.author: kekqqq
children.textRawHtml: I also know a company that fits this very well. I wonder how many such companies are out there.
children.textZhHtml: 我也知道一家非常适合这一点的公司。我想知道这样的公司有多少家。

children.author: thih9
children.textRawHtml:
In a one electron universe[1] it might actually be the same company (with a very productive sales team).

[1]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/One-electron_universe
children.textZhHtml:
在单电子宇宙[1]中，它们实际上可能是同一家公司（拥有一支极其高效的销售团队）。

[1]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/One-electron_universe

children.author: smallnix
children.textRawHtml:
> I’ve sat through some pitches like that, where it is very obvious that the founder/CEO has limited knowledge and expertise in what they’re pitching, where the product is limited, but their enthusiasm is off the charts.

And for all the talk of investing into people, what was your opinion?
children.textZhHtml:
> 我参加过一些类似的推介活动，很明显，创始人/首席执行官对他们所推介的产品的知识和专业知识有限，产品也有限，但他们的热情却高得离谱。

对于所有关于投资于人的讨论，您有何看法？

children.author: lionkor
children.textRawHtml: Not OP, but overconfidence is not a quality I would invest in.
children.textZhHtml: 我不是楼主，但过度自信可不是我愿意投资的一种品质。

children.author: sph
children.textRawHtml: You must not be from California

children.author: latexr
children.textRawHtml: Would you invest in a person with limited knowledge and expertise just because they’re enthusiastic? Might as well hire a literal clown; all the enthusiasm you might want and way cheaper.
children.textZhHtml: 如果一个人知识有限、缺乏专业技能，仅仅因为他热情高涨，你会投资他吗？那还不如直接雇个小丑呢；热情绝对管够，而且便宜得多。

children.author: pseudohadamard
children.textRawHtml:
> The founder’s motivation. To become wealthy.

Or, if they're an engineer, to design cool products while not having the faintest idea of how to run a business and manage people. Ran into far more of those than get-rich-quick types.

Having said that, I was also lucky enough to work for a company where the founder had a science degree but not in the field he was currently working in, was really good at sales, and recognised that others knew a lot more about the field than he did and was willing to listen to them. One of the lead tech guys could literally say to him "this is a stupid idea" and the idea would get changed. They muddled along for some years until he found the right people to run things, and have been doing pretty well ever since. Last I checked it was still a good place to work for.
children.textZhHtml:
> 创始人的动机。变得富有。

或者，如果他们是一名工程师，则可以设计很酷的产品，但对如何经营企业和管理人员一无所知。遇到的这类人比快速致富的类型要多得多。

话虽如此，我也很幸运能够在一家公司工作，该公司的创始人拥有科学学位，但不是他目前从事的领域，他非常擅长销售，并且认识到其他人比他更了解该领域，并且愿意倾听他们的意见。一位首席技术人员可以对他说“这是一个愚蠢的想法”，这个想法就会改变。他们混日子了好几年，直到他找到了合适的人来管理事情，从那以后一直做得很好。最后我检查了一下它仍然是一个工作的好地方。

children.author: overgard
children.textRawHtml: Musk strikes me as a guy that keeps succeeding despite his bad ideas because the system is so rigged that getting wealthy off one good bet can sustain you forever. I mean, his track record this entire decade is proposterously stupid (cybertruck, twitter, everything he says on twitter, the CSAM and deepfake creation engine known as grok, DOGE, etc.). When his companies succeed, its mostly because the people he's hired treat him like a toddler they need to appease while keeping him out of the important things. To me he's not a positive example, he's an embarassment that demonstrates that being rich does not require insight or intelligence.
children.textZhHtml: 马斯克给我的印象是，尽管他的想法很糟糕，但他仍然不断取得成功，因为这个系统被操纵得很严重，从一个好的赌注中致富可以让你永远维持下去。我的意思是，他这整个十年的记录简直愚蠢透顶（cybertruck、twitter、他在 Twitter 上所说的一切、CSAM 和被称为 grok、DOGE 的 Deepfake 创作引擎等）。当他的公司取得成功时，主要是因为他雇用的人对待他就像对待一个蹒跚学步的孩子一样，他们需要安抚他，同时不让他参与重要的事情。对我来说，他不是一个积极的榜样，他是一个尴尬，表明富有并不需要洞察力或智慧。

children.author: mlacks
children.textRawHtml: Why is it that people focus on the negatives. I'm not going to pick apart the whole comment but World's first self-landing rocket ship and best selling electric car in the world are not figments of a "system is rigged that getting wealthy off one good bet can sustain you forever".
children.textZhHtml: 为什么人们总盯着负面因素不放。我不打算逐句反驳这条评论，但全球首枚可自主着陆的火箭和全球最畅销的电动汽车，可不是什么“体制暗箱操作，靠一次好赌就能富贵一生”的臆想产物。

children.author: lobsterthief
children.textRawHtml: You’ve cherry-picked two successful examples but ignored all his failures. He wasn’t even solely responsible for those two successes. He didn’t found Tesla. He didn’t even found PayPal. He’s not an aerospace engineer. The number one predictor of future wealth and success is starting with wealth to begin with.
children.textZhHtml: 你挑了两个成功的例子，却忽略了他所有的失败。他甚至不是那两个成功项目的唯一负责人。他没有创立特斯拉。他连PayPal都不是创始人。他不是航空航天工程师。预测未来财富和成功的首要指标，是一开始就拥有财富。

children.author: Muhammad523
children.textRawHtml:
> World's first self-landing rocket

The Buran, a soviet spaceplane very similar to the space shuttle, landed autonomously
children.textZhHtml:
>世界上第一个自动着陆火箭

暴风雪号，一种与航天飞机非常相似的苏联航天飞机，自主着陆

children.author: overgard
children.textRawHtml:
I focus on the negatives because they're so massive. Elon didn't even start Tesla, he was an investor that forced the founder out. I'll concede that SpaceX is a little better, but even then a lot of his ideas are profoundly stupid (while I think colonizing the solar system is worthwhile, doing it on Mars is very dumb. You'd need to build underground bunkers so people don't die of cancer from radiation, and there aren't that many useful natural resources there. The moon is much more useful from a practical standpoint of creating easier access to the rest of the solar system, and Titan makes a lot more sense for a sustainable colony. But Elon is a toddler so he just went "Mars!!")

Lets throw in a few more of Elon's failures, this time lets focus on the ones that are outright dangerous or killing people!

- In an accident, the Cybertruck has frequently trapped people inside the vehicle and caused them to burn to death when the batteries catch fire because they hid the manual release on the doors for aesthetic reasons, so people couldn't get out. Also the unbreakable glass means that people spent their final moments desperately trying to smash an unbreakable window while burning to death. People have died from this! It was totally predictable and preventable! https://www.ktvu.com/news/parents-woman-killed-cybertruck-cr...

- Fully self driving. Hasn't happened, and a lot of the danger is because he wanted to cheap out on lidar and brashly overpromised on its capabilities.

Lets also focus on some of the funny ones that came from his need for validation, like needing people to think he's a great gamer: https://www.theguardian.com/games/2025/jan/20/elon-musk-stan... (why would any adult not involved in E-Sports even want to convince their peers they're great at gaming? Like, who cares?!)

Even when he has a good idea like Starlink he ruins it by awful about it (shutting it off for entities he disagrees with).

This guy should not be anyone's hero.
children.textZhHtml:
我关注负面因素，因为它们太大了。埃隆甚至没有创办特斯拉，他只是一个迫使创始人退出的投资者。我承认 SpaceX 好一点，但即便如此，他的很多想法仍然非常愚蠢（虽然我认为殖民太阳系是值得的，但在火星上这样做是非常愚蠢的。你需要建造地下掩体，这样人们就不会因辐射而死于癌症，而且那里没有那么多有用的自然资源。从实际的角度来看，月球更有用，可以更容易地进入太阳系的其他部分，而泰坦对于可持续的殖民地来说更有意义。但是Elon 是个小孩，所以他刚刚去了“火星！”）

让我们再举几个 Elon 的失败经历，这次让我们重点关注那些完全危险或致命的失败！

- 在一次事故中，Cybertruck 经常将人困在车内，并在电池起火时导致他们被烧死，因为他们出于美观原因将手动释放装置隐藏在车门上，使人们无法逃脱。而且，牢不可破的玻璃意味着人们在生命的最后时刻拼命地试图砸碎一扇牢不可破的窗户，同时被烧死。人们已经因此而死亡！这是完全可以预见和预防的！ https://www.ktvu.com/news/parents-woman-killed-cybertruck-cr...

- 完全自动驾驶。还没有发生，很多危险是因为他想在激光雷达上便宜，并对其功能做出过分夸大的承诺。

让我们也关注一些来自他需要验证的有趣的事情，比如需要人们认为他是一个伟大的游戏玩家：https://www.theguardian.com/games/2025/jan/20/elon-musk-stan...（为什么任何不参与电子竞技的成年人甚至想说服他们的孩子）他们很擅长游戏？就像，谁在乎？！）

即使他有一个像星链这样的好主意，他也会因为糟糕而毁了它（对他不同意的实体将其关闭）。

这个人不应该成为任何人的英雄。

嘿，感谢您的全面回复。我明白你的观点，但我认为任何一个展示了一生的人都会在成功的同时经历很多失败。对于 FSD，我相信它已经完成了 - 请参阅以下内容： https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JwMlAj1tpQg
你可以只写一句话：“我不喜欢他支持特朗普”。因为这本质上就是反马斯克的立场。与此同时，每条道路上都有一辆特斯拉、太空互联网和自动着陆火箭。

children.author: mlacks
children.textRawHtml: Hey thanks for the throrough response. I see your point, but I think anyone with their whole life on display would have plenty of failures to go with the successes. For FSD, I believe its done - see the following: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JwMlAj1tpQg
children.textZhHtml: 嘿，感谢您的全面回复。我明白你的观点，但我认为任何一个展示了一生的人都会在成功的同时经历很多失败。对于 FSD，我相信它已经完成了 - 请参阅以下内容： https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JwMlAj1tpQg

children.author: cpursley
children.textRawHtml: You could have just written a single sentence: “I don’t like that he supported Trump”. Because that’s essentially what the anti-Musk position is. Meanwhile, there’s a Tesla on every road, space internet and self landing rockets.
children.textZhHtml: 你可以只写一句话：“我不喜欢他支持特朗普”。因为这本质上就是反马斯克的立场。与此同时，每条道路上都有一辆特斯拉、太空互联网和自动着陆火箭。

children.author: m463
children.textRawHtml:
I think Musk actually wasn't in it for the money.

He made all the money first the silicon valley way (paypal) and then decided to actually make and sell non-software/ip things that physically exist. I think that is actually hard. And if successful very valuable.

Although making software isn't easy, I think there are lots of silicon valley types who create IP, but enter a super-competitive market. IP can be valuable, but it might be quick and easy to spool up a competitor and/or copy it.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为马斯克最初其实并不是为了钱。

他先是按硅谷的方式赚到了所有的钱（PayPal），然后决定真正去制造和销售那些物理存在的、非软件或知识产权的实体产品。我认为这其实很难。而且如果成功了，价值极高。

虽然做软件也不容易，但我认为有很多硅谷人士创造知识产权，却进入了竞争极其激烈的市场。知识产权可能很有价值，但可能很快又很容易就能拉起一个竞争对手和/或将其复制。

children.author: taneq
children.textRawHtml: I think he genuinely broke his brain during the Model 3 ramp up. They were in production hell for something like a year, and he hasn’t been quite right ever since. Maybe that was just the point at which he got rich enough to stop caring what other people think, though.
children.textZhHtml: 我觉得他在Model 3产能爬坡期间真的把脑子搞坏了。他们经历了差不多一年的“产能地狱”，从那以后他就不太对劲了。不过，也许那只是他变得足够富有、不再在乎别人看法的一个转折点罢了。

children.author: teiferer
children.textRawHtml:
> The defense is of course that some people can do that - Musk did it, so why not?

If you think that Musk did his endeavors in order to become rich, you are likely mistaken.
children.textZhHtml:
> 当然，辩解是有些人可以做到这一点 - 马斯克做到了，那为什么不呢？

如果你认为马斯克努力是为了致富，那你可能就错了。
